zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard於1518年出生在英國可瓦爾聖 奧斯特爾時，她的父親Sir James Trewinnard of Arwenack27歲，母親Lady Phillippa Carminowe10歲。她與Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle在英國結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1583年11月在英國英格蘭可瓦爾布度可 沃特享年65歲，埋葬在英國可瓦爾布度可 沃特。

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Of uncertain origin. If not simply an invention, it may have been devised as a feminine equivalent of   Alan   (of which it is an anagram), or a shortened form of   Alana .  It seems to have been first used by the American film actress  Lana Turner  (  1920–1995 ), whose original name was  Julia .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard was born in 1518, in St Austell, Cornwall, England, her father, Sir James Trewinnard of Arwenack, was 27 and her mother, Lady Phillippa Carminowe, was 10. She married Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle in England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. She died in November 1583, in Budock Water, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65, and was buried in Budock Water, Cornwall, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard est née en 1518, à St Austell, Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Sir James Trewinnard of Arwenack, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Lady Phillippa Carminowe, avait 10 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée en novembre 1583, à Budock Water, Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Budock Water, Cornouailles, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard 1518 in St Austell, Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir James Trewinnard of Arwenack 27 und ihre Mutter Lady Phillippa Carminowe 10. Sie hat in England Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb im November 1583 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Budock Water, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Budock Water, Cornwall, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Trewinnardが1518年にイギリスコルンワルスト アウステルで生まれた時，父Sir James Trewinnard of Arwenackは27歳，母Lady Phillippa Carminoweは10歳であった。イギリスでSir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1583年11月にイギリスイギリスコルンワルブドック ワターで65歳で死去し，イギリスコルンワルブドック ワターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard(이)가 1518년 영국 코른월 스트 오스텔에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir James Trewinnard of Arwenack(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Phillippa Carminowe(은)는 10세였다. 그녀는 영국에서 Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1583년 11월 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 버독 왓어에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 코른월 버독 왓어에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Trewinnard родилась в 1518 г. в местности ст аустелл, корнвалл, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir James Trewinnard of Arwenack было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Phillippa Carminowe, 10. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Killigrew III First Gov. Pendennis Castle в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Она умерла ноябрь 1583 г. в местности будокк вэитир, корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности будокк вэитир, корнвалл, Англия.
